ddls:
CREATE TABLE "hospitalizations" (
  "hospitalization_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hospitalization record */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the hospitalization data */,
  "hospital_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hospital where the data is recorded */,
  "new_hospitalized" INTEGER /* Number of new hospitalized patients on the given date */,
  "total_hospitalized" INTEGER /* Total number of hospitalized patients on the given date */,
  "total_hospitalized_without_release" INTEGER /* Total number of hospitalized patients without release on the given date */,
  "hard_status_count" INTEGER /* Number of patients in critical (hard) status */,
  "medium_status_count" INTEGER /* Number of patients in moderate (medium) status */,
  "easy_status_count" INTEGER /* Number of patients in mild (easy) status */,
  "breathing_support_count" INTEGER /* Number of patients requiring breathing support */,
  "death_count" INTEGER /* Number of patient deaths on the given date */,
  "total_beds" INTEGER /* Total number of beds available in the hospital */,
  "standard_occupancy" REAL /* Standard occupancy rate of the hospital */,
  "num_visits" INTEGER /* Number of patient visits on the given date */,
  "patients_home" INTEGER /* Number of patients in home care */,
  "patients_hotel" INTEGER /* Number of patients in quarantine hotels */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the record */,
  "icu_count" INTEGER /* Number of patients in the ICU */,
  "ventilator_count" INTEGER /* Number of patients on ventilators */,
  "recovered_count" INTEGER /* Number of patients recovered on the given date */,
  "average_stay_duration" REAL /* Average duration of stay for patients */,
  "staff_shortage_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if there is a staff shortage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hospitalization_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hospitalizations_hospital_id FOREIGN KEY ("hospital_id") REFERENCES hospitals ("hospital_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hospitals" (
  "hospital_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hospital */,
  "hospital_name" TEXT /* Name of the hospital */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the hospital is located */,
  "total_beds" INTEGER /* Total number of beds available in the hospital */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the hospital */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the hospital */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number */,
  "fax_number" TEXT /* Fax number for official communications */,
  "specialties" TEXT /* Specialties offered by the hospital */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hospital_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hospitals_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the region is located */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the region is located */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the region */,
  "area_sq_km" REAL /* Area of the region in square kilometers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patient_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patient status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the patient status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the patient status */,
  "color_code" TEXT /* Color code for visual representation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the user role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the user role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Detailed permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user role */,
  "hospital_id" INTEGER /* ID of the hospital the user is associated with */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_hospital_id FOREIGN KEY ("hospital_id") REFERENCES hospitals ("hospital_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "hospitalization_id" INTEGER /* ID of the hospitalization record being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_hospitalization_id FOREIGN KEY ("hospitalization_id") REFERENCES hospitalizations ("hospitalization_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_validations" (
  "validation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each validation rule */,
  "validation_name" TEXT /* Name of the validation rule */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the validation rule */,
  "rule" TEXT /* Rule definition (e.g., SQL condition) */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Custom error message for failed validations */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("validation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audits" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit record */,
  "hospitalization_id" INTEGER /* ID of the hospitalization record being audited */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user performing the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the action */,
  "details" TEXT /* Details of the action (e.g., old and new values) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the action was performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audits_hospitalization_id FOREIGN KEY ("hospitalization_id") REFERENCES hospitalizations ("hospitalization_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audits_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
